name: Quote
goal: Render a semantic blockquote with optional author and source attribution in three visual treatments: card, pull, and inline.
description: Blockquote component used in research, customer development, B2B reports, and persona documents. Wraps a native <blockquote> and <cite> for correct ARIA semantics. Attribution accepts plain text via attributes (author, source) or rich markup via named slots — links, time elements, chips. Three variants cover the most common corpus patterns: card (purple left-border block), pull (oversized centered pull-quote), and inline (indented prose blockquote).

props.name: author
props.description: Short author name for the simple case. Used as fallback text when slot="author" is empty.

props.name: source
props.description: Short source citation for the simple case. Used as fallback when slot="source" is empty.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual treatment: card (purple left-border block), pull (oversized centered italic), inline (indented italic blockquote, no card chrome).

props.name: lang
props.description: Language tag forwarded to the host element lang= attribute. Useful when the quote is in a different language from the surrounding page.

slots.description: The quote text. REQUIRED. May contain inline rich content: <em>, <a>, <strong>.

slots.name: author
slots.description: Rich author block; overrides the author attribute. Allows links, chips, time elements.

slots.name: source
slots.description: Rich source citation; overrides the source attribute. Allows links, time elements, chips.

cssVariables.name: --ce-space-5
cssVariables.description: Card horizontal padding; pull vertical padding; inline left inset.

cssVariables.name: --ce-text-base
cssVariables.description: Card and inline body font size.

cssVariables.name: --ce-text-xl
cssVariables.description: Pull variant oversized body font size.

a11y.notes: The shadow DOM renders a native <blockquote> element containing the default slot. Attribution is rendered inside a <footer><cite> so screen readers announce it as a citation. The separator dot is aria-hidden.
